text.title: Secret Rotation Drill Runbook Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every secret rotation drill MUST follow to produce audit-grade evidence. Skipping any rule invalidates the drill as a compliance artefact.

rule.statement: The drill MUST target a non-production environment that mirrors prod's consumer topology (same services, same secret aliases, same scheduler jobs). Running against prod = NOT a drill, it's an incident — separate runbook applies.
rule.rationale: A drill that breaks prod customers is malpractice. A clone environment is the only acceptable target; if no clone exists, the prerequisite is to build one first.

rule.statement: The drill MUST include a scenario where the rotation system itself is unavailable. Operator MUST retrieve out-of-band breakglass credentials, perform manual rotation, and document the path. Drill records without a breakglass scenario are INVALID as compliance evidence.
rule.rationale: Real incidents that trigger emergency rotation often coincide with rotation-system outage (compromised Vault, AWS region down). Auditors specifically ask "what if Vault is offline" — drilling the answer is the point.

rule.statement: The drill MUST record MTTR (Mean Time To Rotation) per consumer category: API service, scheduler, database, IaC pipeline. Operators MUST start the clock at "rotation triggered" and stop it at "consumer verified using new value." Unmeasured drills = INVALID.
rule.rationale: Without a numeric MTTR, the org can't tell if rotation capability is improving or regressing. Auditors trend MTTR over multiple drills.

rule.statement: After the drill, the operator MUST verify the non-prod environment is back to a known-good state, all rotated secrets are usable, and no orphan secrets remain in the store. Restoration checklist signed off = drill complete; unsigned = drill OPEN.
rule.rationale: Half-completed drills leave the clone in a worse state than they found it; orphan secrets are themselves a finding. Restoration is part of the drill, not an after-the-fact concern.

rule.statement: The drill MUST emit a signed evidence pack containing: pre-drill checklist, rotation timeline (timestamps + operator actions), breakglass scenario log, MTTR per consumer category, restoration sign-off, and reviewer signature. Pack stored in compliance evidence vault with retention ≥ 7 years.
rule.rationale: SOC2 Type II and ISO 27001 specifically ask for dated, signed evidence of rotation. Verbal "yes we drill quarterly" is not acceptable evidence.

rule.statement: Drills MUST run on a quarterly cadence at minimum; a real rotation event with full evidence pack counts as the drill for that quarter. Two consecutive quarters with no drill = compliance escalation, not "we'll do it next sprint."
rule.rationale: Annual drills are too rare to catch consumer drift; monthly is operator-fatigue territory. Quarterly is the auditor-accepted minimum.

example.title: Evidence pack header
example.text: drill_id: Q2-2026-stripe target_env: staging-clone operator: @ops-lead reviewer: @ciso rotation_started: 2026-05-20T09:00Z rotation_complete: 2026-05-20T09:18Z mttr: {api: 4m12s, scheduler: 8m03s, db: 11m45s, iac: 6m31s} breakglass_exercised: true restoration_signoff: 2026-05-20T11:00Z by @ops-lead evidence_pack: vault://compliance/drills/2026-Q2-stripe.tar.gz

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
